name: English Crumpets
recipeInstructions:
English crumpets are delicious when spread hot with butter. The butter
soaks into the crumpet and drips down your chin as you bite them.
Strong white flour is the type bakers use for bread dough. It has a high gluten content, making it ideal for a good risen batter or dough. In England, we have 'Crumpet Rings' which are metal molds about 3 inches in diameter. These are placed into a frying pan and act as molds while the crumpet cooks. Crumpets can be anywhere between 2 inches and 5 inches in diameter.
1. Sift the flour and salt into a mixing bowl.
2. Gently warm the milk to just hand hot and sprinkle on the dried yeast. Leave it to stand for 10 or 15 minutes until frothy.
3. Add the yeast mixture to the flour and beat to a smooth batter. Cover with a damp cloth and leave to stand in a warm place for 45 minutes, or until the batter has doubled in size.
4. Dissolve the bicarb of soda in 15ml (1 tbsp) of warm water and beat it into the batter. Cover again and leave to stand for an additional 20 minutes.
5. Place a 3-inch metal pastry cutter into a hot, greased frying pan. Pour about one tablespoon of batter onto the base, spreading it thinly.
6. Cook until the top is set and the bubbles have burst. Remove from the ring and turn over, cooking the other side for 2 or 3 minutes only. It should just color slightly.
7. Cool on a wire rack. Eat them immediately or leave to go cold and toast them until brown on both sides. Spread with lots of good butter and enjoy.
